name: Trusted HTML Behavior Demo
summary: Attribute behavior that routes an element's innerHTML assignments through a Trusted Types policy (the TrustedHTML sink) instead of writing a raw string to the DOM.
description:
Concept

The trusted-html attribute is the composable behavior form of the Trusted Types API. Put it on any element and every string assignment to that element's innerHTML is routed through a TrustedHTMLPolicy.createHTML(...) sink before it reaches the DOM — making the sink CSP-compatible (require-trusted-types-for 'script') and auditable.

Key points

  • Behavior, not element: hardens the innerHTML sink of an existing element (attribute on any element), distinct from the trusted-html element form it composes with (bias-toward-separation).
  • Native-first / graceful degradation: when Trusted Types is unavailable (Firefox/Safari today, non-secure contexts) the behavior is a transparent pass-through — assignments still work, they are just not routed through a TrustedHTML object.
  • Shared policy: one policy per name, deduped process-wide; an app can pre-register a same-named sanitizing policy and the behavior reuses it.
  • Side-by-side contrast: the demo shows a hardened sink (with the attribute) next to a plain sink (native innerHTML).
